parameters.content:
## eBook Ingestion Overall steps:
1. Download the book
2. Convert the PDF to HTML (IDR)
2.1
3. Generate Thumbs (Acrobat 300px)
4. Extract the compressed file.
6. Cleanup 
6.1 Delete files:
- search
- index
- config
- annotations
- thumbnails

1-many folder is the asset folder
1-many HTML is the HTML page
Font

B. Book to thumbnails 
- All pages image extraction (300px per page)
- All extracted images naming - From ascending order 1-All [000-000] (All)

HTML, 1-many asset folder renaming (000-000)

C. Classification
- How to extract Chapter information from the PDF?
- Create Chapter folder - Eg. Chapter 1 Basic of Sciences (1-25) Maybe Extract from Sheets?
- Page 1-25 asset folder, HTML, Thumbnails & Font folder.

D. Preprocessing the book

E. Content upload (Seeding) After filling in the details

F. Asset upload using book ID from publishing.

G. Cleanup at publishing after assets have been uploaded.
- Update chapter name
- Skip page number
- Update page numbers according to the book
- First chapter feature by looking at the book/ page numbers at the footer (Left right alignment)
- Cover page upload (600px)
- QC from publishing 

H. Pull book to BO and link to platform. Update sheet!

## Starting steps:
1. Download the book
2. Convert the PDF to HTML (IDR)
2.1
3. Generate Thumbs (Acrobat 300px)
4. Extract the compressed file.
6. Cleanup 
6.1 Delete files:
- search
- index
- config
- annotations
- thumbnails

1-many folder is the asset folder
1-many HTML is the HTML page
Font

B. Book to thumbnails 
- All pages image extraction (300px per page)
- All extracted images naming - From ascending order 1-All [000-000] (All)

HTML, 1-many asset folder renaming (000-000)

C. Classification
- How to extract Chapter information from the PDF?
- Create Chapter folder - Eg. Chapter 1 Basic of Sciences (1-25) Maybe Extract from Sheets?
- Page 1-25 asset folder, HTML, Thumbnails & Font folder.

parameters.command:
=# 1. Setup paths
BASE_DIR="/data/output"
ARCHIVE_DIR="$BASE_DIR/archive"
TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M)

mkdir -p "$ARCHIVE_DIR"

# 2. Find newest zip
cd "$BASE_DIR"
LATEST_ZIP=$(ls -t *.zip 2>/dev/null | head -1)

if [ -z "$LATEST_ZIP" ]; then
  echo '{"error": "No zip file found"}'
  exit 1
fi

FOLDER_NAME="${LATEST_ZIP%.*}"
TARGET_DIR="$BASE_DIR/$FOLDER_NAME"

# Avoid overwrite
if [ -d "$TARGET_DIR" ]; then
  TARGET_DIR="${TARGET_DIR}_$TIMESTAMP"
fi

mkdir -p "$TARGET_DIR"

# 3. Unzip and Cleanup
unzip -qo "$LATEST_ZIP" -d "$TARGET_DIR" > /dev/null 2>&1

cd "$TARGET_DIR"
rm -rf search* index* config* annotations* thumbnails* > /dev/null 2>&1

# 4. RENAME FOLDERS & HTML (Removed 000 padding)
# Handles 001 -> 1, 025 -> 25, etc.
for f in [0-9] [0-9][0-9] [0-9][0-9][0-9]; do
  # Rename Folders
  if [ -d "$f" ]; then
    # Strip leading zeros
    new_d=$(echo "$f" | sed 's/^0*//')
    [ -z "$new_d" ] && new_d="0" # handle case if folder was named 000
    [ "$f" != "$new_d" ] && mv "$f" "$new_d" > /dev/null 2>&1
  fi
  
  # Rename HTML Files
  html_file="$f.html"
  if [ -f "$html_file" ]; then
    new_f=$(echo "$f" | sed 's/^0*//').html
    [ "$html_file" != "$new_f" ] && mv "$html_file" "$new_f" > /dev/null 2>&1
  fi
done

# 5. Handle PDF
PDF_MATCH="$BASE_DIR/$FOLDER_NAME.pdf"
[ ! -f "$PDF_MATCH" ] && PDF_MATCH="$BASE_DIR/$FOLDER_NAME.PDF"
if [ -f "$PDF_MATCH" ]; then
  mv "$PDF_MATCH" "$TARGET_DIR/" > /dev/null 2>&1
fi

# 6. Archive Zip
mv "$BASE_DIR/$LATEST_ZIP" "$ARCHIVE_DIR/" > /dev/null 2>&1

# 7. RETURN ONLY JSON
echo "{\"folderName\":\"$FOLDER_NAME\", \"targetDir\":\"$TARGET_DIR\"}"

=# 1. Grab directory
TARGET_DIR="{{ JSON.parse($node["Execute Command1"].json.stdout).targetDir }}"

# 2. Find PDF
PDF_FILE=$(find "$TARGET_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -name "*.pdf" -o -name "*.PDF" | head -1)

if [ -z "$PDF_FILE" ]; then
  echo "{\"status\": \"error\", \"msg\": \"No PDF found\"}"
  exit 1
fi

# 3. Enter directory and use local names
cd "$TARGET_DIR"
cp "$PDF_FILE" ./input.pdf

# 4. Run pdftoppm (Removed -pad 3)
/usr/bin/pdftoppm -jpeg -r 300 -jpegopt quality=95 input.pdf page

# 5. Cleanup and Rename
rm -f input.pdf

if ls page-*.jpg >/dev/null 2>&1; then
  for thumb in page-*.jpg; do
    # Strip 'page-' prefix
    raw_name="${thumb#page-}"
    # Strip leading zeros from the number (001.jpg -> 1.jpg)
    new_name=$(echo "$raw_name" | sed 's/^0*//')
    # If the file was 000.jpg, sed makes it empty, so we fix that:
    [ "$new_name" == ".jpg" ] && new_name="0.jpg"
    
    mv "$thumb" "$new_name" > /dev/null 2>&1
  done
  echo "{\"status\": \"success\", \"msg\": \"Thumbnails generated without padding\"}"
else
  echo "{\"status\": \"error\", \"msg\": \"No JPEGs created\"}"
  exit 1
fi

=# 1. Variables
BOOK_ID="{{ JSON.parse($node["Execute Command1"].json.stdout).folderName }}"
TITLE="{{ $json["ChapterTitle"] }}"
START={{ $json["StartPage"] }}
END={{ $json["EndPage"] }}

BOOK_PATH="/data/output/$BOOK_ID"
CHAPTER_PATH="$BOOK_PATH/$TITLE"

# 2. Create Chapter Folder
mkdir -p "$CHAPTER_PATH"

# 3. Move unique assets (HTML, JPG, Asset Folders)
for i in $(seq $START $END); do
  [ -f "$BOOK_PATH/$i.html" ] && mv "$BOOK_PATH/$i.html" "$CHAPTER_PATH/"
  [ -f "$BOOK_PATH/$i.jpg" ] && mv "$BOOK_PATH/$i.jpg" "$CHAPTER_PATH/"
  [ -d "$BOOK_PATH/$i" ] && mv "$BOOK_PATH/$i" "$CHAPTER_PATH/"
done

# 4. Handle Fonts (Copy, don't move, to avoid breaking other chapters)
if [ -d "$BOOK_PATH/fonts" ]; then
  cp -r "$BOOK_PATH/fonts" "$CHAPTER_PATH/"
fi

# 5. Handle PDF (Copy from root to chapter)
PDF_NAME="$BOOK_ID.pdf"
if [ -f "$BOOK_PATH/$PDF_NAME" ]; then
  cp "$BOOK_PATH/$PDF_NAME" "$CHAPTER_PATH/"
fi

echo "{\"status\": \"success\", \"chapter\": \"$TITLE\"}"

=# 1. Setup paths
BASE_DIR="/data/output"
ARCHIVE_DIR="$BASE_DIR/archive"
TIMESTAMP=$(date +%Y-%m-%d_%H-%M)

# 2. Ensure archive folder exists
mkdir -p "$ARCHIVE_DIR"

# 3. Find the newest zip file
cd "$BASE_DIR"
LATEST_ZIP=$(ls -t *.zip 2>/dev/null | head -1)

if [ -z "$LATEST_ZIP" ]; then
  echo "No zip file found"
  exit 1
fi

# 4. Create a folder name
FOLDER_NAME="${LATEST_ZIP%.*}"

# SAFETY CHECK: If folder exists, add timestamp to avoid overwriting
if [ -d "$FOLDER_NAME" ]; then
  TARGET_DIR="$BASE_DIR/${FOLDER_NAME}_$TIMESTAMP"
else
  TARGET_DIR="$BASE_DIR/$FOLDER_NAME"
fi

mkdir -p "$TARGET_DIR"

# 5. Unzip into target
unzip -qo "$LATEST_ZIP" -d "$TARGET_DIR"

# 6. Cleanup & Rename inside the new folder
cd "$TARGET_DIR"
rm -rf search* index* config* annotations* thumbnails*

# Rename Folders (1 -> 001)
for d in [0-9] [0-9][0-9]; do
  if [ -d "$d" ]; then
    mv "$d" "$(printf "%03d" "$d")"
  fi
done

# Rename HTML Files (1.html -> 001.html)
for f in [0-9].html [0-9][0-9].html; do
  if [ -f "$f" ]; then
    base="${f%.html}"
    mv "$f" "$(printf "%03d" "$base").html"
  fi
done

# 7. Move original zip to archive (Safely)
# If zip exists in archive, rename the new one with a timestamp
if [ -f "$ARCHIVE_DIR/$LATEST_ZIP" ]; then
  mv "$BASE_DIR/$LATEST_ZIP" "$ARCHIVE_DIR/${LATEST_ZIP%.*}_$TIMESTAMP.zip"
else
  mv "$BASE_DIR/$LATEST_ZIP" "$ARCHIVE_DIR/"
fi

# ... (after unzip and folder renaming) ...

# NEW PDF LOGIC:
# Find the PDF in the base directory that matches the zip name
PDF_MATCH="$BASE_DIR/$FOLDER_NAME.pdf"

if [ -f "$PDF_MATCH" ]; then
  echo "Matching PDF found. Moving into workspace..."
  mv "$PDF_MATCH" "$TARGET_DIR/"
else
  # Check for lowercase .pdf just in case
  PDF_MATCH_LOWER="$BASE_DIR/$FOLDER_NAME.PDF"
  if [ -f "$PDF_MATCH_LOWER" ]; then
    mv "$PDF_MATCH_LOWER" "$TARGET_DIR/"
  fi
fi

# ... (continue to zip move/archive logic) ...

# Clear any previous text so only the JSON remains
clear > /dev/null
echo "{\"folderName\":\"$FOLDER_NAME\", \"targetDir\":\"$TARGET_DIR\"}"

=set -e

# 1. Grab directory
TARGET_DIR="{{ JSON.parse($node["Execute Command"].json.stdout.match(/\{.*\}/)[0]).targetDir }}"

# 2. Find PDF
PDF_FILE=$(find "$TARGET_DIR" -maxdepth 1 -name "*.pdf" -o -name "*.PDF" | head -1)

if [ -z "$PDF_FILE" ]; then
  echo "No PDF found in $TARGET_DIR"
  exit 1
fi

# 3. Generate Thumbnails using the absolute path we verified
/usr/bin/pdftoppm -jpeg -scale-to 300 -sep "-" -pad 3 "$PDF_FILE" "$TARGET_DIR/page"

# 4. Rename
cd "$TARGET_DIR"
for thumb in page-*.jpg; do
  [ -f "$thumb" ] && mv "$thumb" "${thumb#page-}"
done

echo "Success: Thumbnails generated in $TARGET_DIR"

=# Variables from the Google Sheet row
BOOK="{{ $node["Execute Command"].json.folderName }}"
TITLE="{{ $json["ChapterTitle"] }}"
START={{ $json["StartPage"] }}
END={{ $json["EndPage"] }}

# 1. Path setup
BOOK_PATH="/data/output/$BOOK"
CHAPTER_PATH="$BOOK_PATH/$TITLE"

# 2. Create Chapter Folder
mkdir -p "$CHAPTER_PATH"

# 3. Move the pages in the range
# seq handles the 1 to 1 or 1 to 25 range perfectly
for i in $(seq $START $END); do
  PADDED=$(printf "%03d" $i)
  
  # Move HTML, Thumbnail, and the IDR asset folder
  mv "$BOOK_PATH/$PADDED.html" "$CHAPTER_PATH/" 2>/dev/null
  mv "$BOOK_PATH/$PADDED.jpg" "$CHAPTER_PATH/" 2>/dev/null
  mv "$BOOK_PATH/$PADDED" "$CHAPTER_PATH/" 2>/dev/null
done

# 4. Copy the fonts folder so the chapter is self-contained
cp -r "$BOOK_PATH/fonts" "$CHAPTER_PATH/" 2>/dev/null

=# 1. Variables
BOOK_ID="{{ JSON.parse($node["Execute Command1"].json.stdout).folderName }}"
BOOK_PATH="/data/output/$BOOK_ID"

# 2. Cleanup
if [ -d "$BOOK_PATH" ]; then
  # Delete only the PDF sitting in the root (not inside chapters)
  find "$BOOK_PATH" -maxdepth 1 \( -name "*.pdf" -o -name "*.PDF" \) -delete
fi

echo "{\"status\": \"complete\", \"msg\": \"Root cleaned for $BOOK_ID\"}"
